name: spells
description: 4-player party arena brawler with power cards. MVP: 2 classes (Wizard+Warrior), 1 arena, core movement, basic projectile, parry, 8 cards. Camera zoom round timer. Damage over time with boundary.

recent_commits: 832dadf Initial commit: core movement system with slope physics

PlayerController.cs.purpose: Core movement physics — MoveHorizontal, gravity, jump, wave-land, wall slide, dash burst

MovementData.cs.purpose: ScriptableObject with all tunable movement parameters

PlayerStateMachine.cs.purpose: State machine — manages GroundedState, AirborneState, WallSlidingState. Shared timers.

GroundedState.cs.purpose: Ground movement, jump, wave-land grace timer, simple slope anti-slide

AirborneState.cs.purpose: Air control, variable jump height, coyote time, wall slide entry, 3-zone gravity

WallSlidingState.cs.purpose: Wall slide with accelerating fall speed, gravity reduction

PhysicsCheck.cs.purpose: Ground/wall/ceiling detection, slope detection, layer auto-detection

PlayerInputHandler.cs.purpose: Unity Input System bridge — implements IInputProvider

TestArenaBuilder.cs.purpose: Procedural test arena — ramps, bowl pit, pillars, platforms

decisions.text: Switched from speedDiff*rate acceleration to Mathf.MoveTowards — old formula reached max speed in 1 frame
Zeroed physics material friction — all movement is code-controlled
Removed MoveOnSlope and CounterSlopeGravity — replaced with simple anti-slide (zero velocity when idle on slope)
Added 3-zone gravity: fall (2.5x), peak-of-jump (0.4x with jump held), rising-no-jump (2.5x)
Increased waveLandFriction 6→30 to compensate for zero physics friction

Jump tuning recommendation: jumpForce 12 (apex ~2.45u) addresses 'jumps too high' feedback. With peak gravity 0.4x + coyote 0.12s + jump buffer 0.12s, the jump should feel responsive but not floaty. Alternative: jumpForce 11 (apex ~2.05u) if 12 still feels too high.

pending.text: Test movement physics changes — validate compilation and in-game feel
Tune jump arc — Britton says jumps may still be too high
Verify wave-land works with zero friction
Implement parry system (next after movement is solid)

notes.text: Britton wants basic platform fighter feel — Melee, Celeste, Mario references. Fluid and immediately graspable.
Unity 6000.3.11f1 installed on this Mac — can compile and test here, not just Windows.

Jump arc analysis: jumpForce 14→12 lowers apex from ~3.3 to ~2.45 units. Formula: h=v²/(2g) where g=gravityScale*9.81=29.43. Closer to Smash Bros full-hop height (~2x character). Suggested change — needs playtesting. Peak gravity (0.4x) extends hang time at apex, so effective perceived height is slightly higher than raw calculation.

External work tools complete (2026-03-15): project_context, code_validate, change_tracker, code_patterns, test_harness, refactor_tool all tested on Spells. 7 EditMode tests passing. Spells.asmdef created. Ready for real game development work.
